title: astrantia major var. pyrenaica
intro: Astrantia major var. pyrenaica, also known as the Pyrenean masterwort, is a flowering plant species in the carrot family (Apiaceae). It is native to the Pyrenees mountains and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote digestion, making it a valuable addition to herbal remedies. Astrantia major var. pyrenaica can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, allowing for various applications in traditional medicine and natural health practices.
